stem: A hemisphere-shaped bowl has an outer radius of 10 centimeters and a uniform thickness of 1 centimeter. What is the volume of the material that makes up the bowl, in cubic centimeters?
explanation: The outer hemisphere volume is $\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{4}{3}\pi(10)^3 = \frac{2}{3}\pi(1000) = \frac{2000\pi}{3}$. The inner hemisphere has radius $10 - 1 = 9$, so its volume is $\frac{2}{3}\pi(9)^3 = \frac{2}{3}\pi(729) = \frac{1458\pi}{3}$. Material volume: $\frac{2000\pi}{3} - \frac{1458\pi}{3} = \frac{542\pi}{3}$. The answer is A.

distractorRationale.B: Computes only the outer hemisphere volume without subtracting the inner: $\frac{2}{3}\pi(10)^3 = \frac{2000\pi}{3}$.
distractorRationale.C: Computes only the inner hemisphere volume instead of the material: $\frac{2}{3}\pi(9)^3 = \frac{1458\pi}{3}$.
distractorRationale.D: Doubles the correct answer by computing full sphere shells instead of hemispheres: $2 \times \frac{542\pi}{3} = \frac{1084\pi}{3}$.
